title: Catch Semantics, Collection Identity, Randomness, and Deterministic Evidence · Execution Trace: Pokedex Go Collection Identity
summary: Catch uses a hidden global random source, stores partial response pointers, duplicates the same Pokemon unpredictably, and makes tests depend on luck and request timing. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go collection identity increment in deterministic owned Pokemon collection transition. Learners progress through prediction, ex…
objectives: Key owned entries by resolved stable ID while retaining display name and capture evidence without raw-input duplicates.
If probability is used, inject a narrow source, record seed or draw evidence, bound values, and keep policy separate from randomness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

theory case — A theory Go team handles case 4572 during a fair-use API stewardship review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile deterministic catch decisions with stable identity and unchanged duplicate state; no user-visible meaning may rely on color or cursor motion; then change one resource identity, pagination link, JSON field, status, or body budget and revalidate admission. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
